title: Actinophloeus angustifolius
intro: Actinophloeus angustifolius, also known as Brazilian cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Brazil. The health benefits of Actinophloeus angustifolius include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, limonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Actinophloeus angustifolius include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Actinophloeus angustifolius is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family is part of the order Sapindales, which also includes other families such as Anacardiaceae and Meliaceae. The Rutaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, with a wide range of plant species. Actinophloeus angustifolius is a species of flowering plant native to Australia and is often found in coastal areas and woodlands. It is a small tree or shrub with a compact growth habit and is often used in horticulture for its attractive foliage.
